items.title: Fallbrook Estate
items.shortDescription: An empty California estate fully imagined — staged for the listing camera and the open-house walkthrough alike.
items.longDescription: A sprawling vacant home in Fallbrook hills, dressed top to bottom with a warm, livable luxury palette tuned to attract buyers in the upper segment. Brass pendants, neutral textiles, layered art, and a tight three-week inventory rolling rental kept the home camera-ready through the entire listing window.

items.title: Menifee Family Home
items.shortDescription: A young family's first forever home — designed to grow with the kids, the dog, and the way life actually moves through a house.
items.longDescription: A full-service interior design project for a young family in Menifee, blending durable performance fabrics with editorial-grade composition. Cream sectionals, warm plank flooring, marble accents, and intentional negative space create rooms that feel collected, considered, and quietly elevated — without sacrificing the rough-and-tumble realities of daily family life.

items.title: Temeku Hills Golf Estate
items.shortDescription: An occupied home in Temecula's Temeku Hills, edited and elevated for the listing without disrupting the family's daily routine.
items.longDescription: A discreet occupied-staging project on a fairway-facing lot, working with the homeowner's existing furniture and adding curated accessories, art, and styling layers to translate the home cleanly to the listing photography. Black dining surfaces, brass details, and seasonal florals tied the spaces together while staying respectful of the family's daily flow.
